heading: Telehealth network and telehealth resource centers grant programs
label: 254c–14 Telehealth network and telehealth resource centers grant programs

Guidance on Furnishing Services Via Telehealth to Individuals With Limited English Proficiency, , , provided that:Pub. L. 119–75, div. J, title II, § 6213140 Stat. 653Feb. 3, 2026“(a).—In GeneralNot later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this section [], the Secretary of Health and Human Services, in consultation with 1 or more entities from each of the categories described in paragraphs (1) through (7) of subsection (b), shall issue and disseminate, or update and revise as applicable, guidance for the entities described in such subsection on the following:Feb. 3, 2026“(1)Best practices on facilitating and integrating use of interpreters during a telemedicine appointment.“(2)Best practices on providing accessible instructions on how to access telecommunications systems (as such term is used for purposes of section 1834(m) of the Social Security Act () for individuals with limited English proficiency.42 U.S.C. 1395m(m)“(3)Best practices on improving access to digital patient portals for individuals with limited English proficiency.“(4)Best practices on integrating the use of video platforms that enable multi-person video calls furnished via a telecommunications system for purposes of providing interpretation during a telemedicine appointment for an individual with limited English proficiency.“(5)Best practices for providing patient materials, communications, and instructions in multiple languages, including text message appointment reminders and prescription information.“(b).—Entities DescribedFor purposes of subsection (a), an entity described in this subsection is an entity in 1 or more of the following categories:“(1)Health information technology service providers, including—“(A)electronic medical record companies;“(B)remote patient monitoring companies; and“(C)telehealth or mobile health vendors and companies.“(2)Health care providers, including—“(A)physicians; and“(B)hospitals.“(3)Health insurers.“(4)Language service companies.“(5)Interpreter or translator professional associations.“(6)Health and language services quality certification organizations.“(7)Patient and consumer advocates, including such advocates that work with individuals with limited English proficiency.”

Guidance on Furnishing Services Via Telehealth to Individuals With Limited English Proficiency, , , provided that:Pub. L. 119–75, div. and“(C)telehealth or mobile health vendors and companies.“(2)Health care providers, including—“(A)physicians; and“(B)hospitals.“(3)Health insurers.“(4)Language service companies.“(5)Interpreter or translator professional associations.“(6)Health and language services quality certification organizations.“(7)Patient and consumer advocates, including such advocates that work with individuals with limited English proficiency.”

children.heading: Director; Office
children.label: 1 Director; Office
children.text: The terms “Director” and “Office” mean the Director and Office specified in subsection (c).

children.heading: Federally qualified health center and rural health clinic
children.label: 2 Federally qualified health center and rural health clinic
children.text: The term “Federally qualified health center” and “rural health clinic” have the meanings given the terms in .section 1395x(aa) of this title

children.heading: Frontier community
children.label: 3 Frontier community
children.text: The term “frontier community” shall have the meaning given the term in regulations issued under subsection (r).

children.heading: Medically underserved area
children.label: 4 Medically underserved area
children.text: The term “medically underserved area” has the meaning given the term “medically underserved community” in .section 295p(6) of this title

children.heading: Medically underserved population
children.label: 5 Medically underserved population
children.text: The term “medically underserved population” has the meaning given the term in .section 254b(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: Telehealth services
children.label: 6 Telehealth services
children.text: The term “telehealth services” means services provided through telehealth technologies.

children.heading: Telehealth technologies
children.label: 7 Telehealth technologies
children.text: The term “telehealth technologies” means technologies relating to the use of electronic information, and telecommunications technologies, to support and promote, at a distance, health care, patient and professional health-related education, health administration, and public health.

children.heading: Programs
children.label: b Programs
children.text: The Secretary shall establish, under , telehealth network and telehealth resource centers grant programs.section 241 of this title

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Health Resources and Services Administration an Office for the Advancement of Telehealth. The Office shall be headed by a Director.

children.heading: Duties
children.label: 2 Duties
children.text: The telehealth network and telehealth resource centers grant programs established under shall be administered by the Director, in consultation with the State offices of rural health, State offices concerning primary care, or other appropriate State government entities.section 241 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: expand access to, coordinate, and improve access to, and the quality of, health care services; and

children.label: B
children.text: expand and improve the quality of health information available to health care providers,, patients, and their families.1So in original.1

children.heading: Telehealth resource centers grants
children.label: 2 Telehealth resource centers grants
children.text: The Director may, in carrying out the telehealth resource centers grant program referred to in subsection (b), award grants to eligible entities for projects to support initiatives that utilize telehealth technologies in the areas and communities, and for the populations, described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Grant periods
children.label: e Grant periods
children.text: The Director may award grants under this section for periods of not more than 5 years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under subsection (d)(1), an entity shall demonstrate that the entity will provide services through a telehealth network.

children.heading: Nature of entities
children.label: 2 Nature of entities
children.text: Each entity participating in the telehealth network may be a nonprofit or for-profit entity.

children.label: A
children.text: Community or migrant health centers or other Federally qualified health centers.

children.label: B
children.text: Health care providers, including pharmacists, in private practice.

children.label: C
children.text: Entities operating clinics, including rural health clinics.

children.label: D
children.text: Local health departments.

children.label: E
children.text: Nonprofit hospitals, including community access hospitals.

children.label: F
children.text: Other publicly funded health or social service agencies.

children.label: G
children.text: Long-term care providers.

children.label: H
children.text: Providers of health care services in the home.

children.label: I
children.text: Providers of outpatient mental health and substance use disorder services and entities operating outpatient mental health and substance use disorder facilities.

children.label: J
children.text: Local or regional emergency health care providers.

children.label: K
children.text: Institutions of higher education.

children.label: L
children.text: Entities operating dental clinics.

children.label: M
children.text: Providers of prenatal, labor care, birthing, and postpartum care services, including hospitals that operate obstetric care units.
a description of the project that the eligible entity will carry out using the funds provided under the grant;

a description of the manner in which the project funded under the grant will meet the health care needs of rural or other populations to be served through the project, and improve the access to services of, and the quality of the services received by, those populations;
evidence of local support for the project, and a description of how the areas, communities, or populations to be served will be involved in the development and ongoing operations of the project;
a plan for sustaining the project after Federal support for the project has ended;
information on the source and amount of non-Federal funds that the entity will provide for the project;
information demonstrating the long-term viability of the project, and other evidence of institutional commitment of the entity to the project;

in the case of an application for a project involving a telehealth network, information demonstrating how the project will promote the integration of telehealth technologies into the operations of health care providers, to avoid redundancy, and improve access to and the quality of care; and
other such information as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Organization
children.label: A Organization
children.text: The eligible entity is a rural community-based organization or another community-based organization.

children.heading: Services
children.label: B Services
children.text: The eligible entity proposes to use Federal funds made available through such a grant to develop plans for, or to establish, telehealth networks that provide mental health care, public health services, long-term care, home care, preventive care, case management services, prenatal care, labor care, birthing care, or postpartum care.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: C Coordination
children.text: The eligible entity demonstrates how the project to be carried out under the grant will be coordinated with other relevant federally funded projects in the areas, communities, and populations to be served through the grant.

children.label: i
children.text: provides clinical health care services, or educational services for health care providers and for patients or their families; and

children.label: I
children.text: a public library;

children.label: II
children.text: an institution of higher education; or

children.label: III
children.text: a local government entity.

children.heading: Connectivity
children.label: E Connectivity
children.text: The eligible entity proposes a project that promotes local and regional connectivity within areas, communities, or populations to be served through the project.

children.heading: Provision of services
children.label: A Provision of services
children.text: The eligible entity has a record of success in the provision of telehealth services to rural areas, medically underserved areas, or medically underserved populations.

children.heading: Collaboration and sharing of expertise
children.label: B Collaboration and sharing of expertise
children.text: The eligible entity has a demonstrated record of collaborating and sharing expertise with providers of telehealth services at the national, regional, State, and local levels.

children.heading: Broad range of telehealth services
children.label: C Broad range of telehealth services
i
children.text: a variety of clinical specialty services;

children.label: ii
children.text: patient or family education;

children.label: iii
children.text: health care professional education; and

children.label: iv
children.text: rural residency support programs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In awarding grants under this section, the Director shall ensure, to the greatest extent possible, that such grants are equitably distributed among the geographical regions of the United States.

children.heading: Telehealth networks
children.label: 2 Telehealth networks
children.text: In awarding grants under subsection (d)(1) for a fiscal year, the Director shall ensure that not less than 50 percent of the funds awarded shall be awarded for projects in rural areas.

children.label: A
children.text: developing and delivering clinical telehealth services that enhance access to community-based health care services in rural areas, frontier communities, or medically underserved areas, or for medically underserved populations;

children.label: B
children.text: developing and acquiring, through lease or purchase, equipment that furthers the objectives of the telehealth network grant program;

children.label: i
children.text: developing and providing distance education, in a manner that enhances access to care in rural areas, frontier communities, or medically underserved areas, or for medically underserved populations; or

children.label: ii
children.text: mentoring, precepting, or supervising health care providers and students seeking to become health care providers, in a manner that enhances access to care in the areas and communities, or for the populations, described in clause (i);

children.label: D
children.text: developing and acquiring instructional programming;

children.label: i
children.text: providing for transmission of medical data, and maintenance of equipment; and

children.label: ii
children.text: providing for compensation (including travel expenses) of specialists, and referring health care providers, who are providing telehealth services through the telehealth network, if no third party payment is available for the telehealth services delivered through the telehealth network;

children.label: F
children.text: developing projects to use telehealth technology to facilitate collaboration between health care providers;

children.label: G
children.text: collecting and analyzing usage statistics and data to document the cost-effectiveness of the telehealth services; and

children.label: H
children.text: carrying out such other activities as are consistent with achieving the objectives of this section, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: providing technical assistance, training, and support, and providing for travel expenses, for health care providers and a range of health care entities that provide or will provide telehealth services;

children.label: B
children.text: disseminating information and research findings related to telehealth services;

children.label: C
children.text: promoting effective collaboration among telehealth resource centers and the Office;

children.label: D
children.text: conducting evaluations to determine the best utilization of telehealth technologies to meet health care needs;

children.label: E
children.text: promoting the integration of the technologies used in clinical information systems with other telehealth technologies;

children.label: F
children.text: fostering the use of telehealth technologies to provide health care information and education for consumers in a more effective manner; and

children.label: G
children.text: implementing special projects or studies under the direction of the Office.
to acquire real property;
for expenditures to purchase or lease equipment, to the extent that the expenditures would exceed 20 percent of the total grant funds;
in the case of a project involving a telehealth network, to purchase or install transmission equipment;
to pay for any equipment or transmission costs not directly related to the purposes for which the grant is awarded;
to purchase or install general purpose voice telephone systems;
for construction; or
for expenditures for indirect costs (as determined by the Secretary), to the extent that the expenditures would exceed 15 percent of the total grant funds.

children.label: A
children.text: are private or public organizations, that receive Federal or State assistance; or

children.label: B
children.text: are public or private entities that operate centers, or carry out programs, that receive Federal or State assistance; and
provide telehealth services or related activities.

children.heading: Coordination with other agencies
children.label: m Coordination with other agencies
children.text: The Secretary shall coordinate activities carried out under grant programs described in subsection (b), to the extent practicable, with Federal and State agencies and nonprofit organizations that are operating similar programs, to maximize the effect of public dollars in funding meritorious proposals.

children.heading: Outreach activities
children.label: n Outreach activities
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and implement procedures to carry out outreach activities to advise potential end users of telehealth services in rural areas, frontier communities, medically underserved areas, and medically underserved populations in each State about the grant programs described in subsection (b).

children.heading: Telehealth
children.label: o Telehealth
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that, for purposes of this section, States should develop reciprocity agreements so that a provider of services under this section who is a licensed or otherwise authorized health care provider under the law of 1 or more States, and who, through telehealth technology, consults with a licensed or otherwise authorized health care provider in another State, is exempt, with respect to such consultation, from any State law of the other State that prohibits such consultation on the basis that the first health care provider is not a licensed or authorized health care provider under the law of that State.

children.heading: Report
children.label: p Report
children.text: Not later than 4 years after , and every 5 years thereafter, the Secretary shall prepare and submit to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives a report on the activities and outcomes of the grant programs under subsection (b).March 27, 2020

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: q Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $29,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025.
